handoff.salientSummary: Implemented always-on-top reinforcement and display-aware placement for the Tauri stage. The main floating window reasserts always-on-top during setup and centers on the monitor under the cursor. Managed windows now center on the main/current monitor when explicit coordinates are absent. Tauri screen commands now expose real monitor and cursor data instead of fixed fake primary-display payloads.
handoff.whatWasImplemented: Added logical display/window placement helpers and tests in apps/stage-tauri/src/window_manager.rs. Startup calls apply_main_window_display_features() to set always_on_top and position the main window on the cursor monitor. open_managed_window() now preserves explicit x/y coordinates and otherwise centers on the main/current monitor work area with HiDPI logical coordinate conversion. Replaced placeholder screen commands in apps/stage-tauri/src/commands/screen.rs with available_monitors(), primary_monitor()/current_monitor() fallback, and cursor_position()/monitor_from_point() conversion. Added Rust tests for placement policy and screen monitor conversion.
handoff.whatWasLeftUndone: Manual always-on-top and multi-display screenshots were not captured. pnpm -F @proj-airi/stage-tauri build and the route Vitest command did not complete because @proj-airi/unplugin-live2d-sdk waited indefinitely while downloading the Cubism SDK on a cold cache; no generated SDK files were written because those paths are not ignored in this worktree.

commandsRun.observation: 17 Rust tests passed with the known placeholder dead-code warnings.
Rust build passed with the known placeholder dead-code warnings.
Rust formatting passed after applying rustfmt.
Interrupted after several minutes idle in vite build. The process printed Downloading Cubism SDK... and then stayed alive with near-zero CPU in @proj-airi/unplugin-live2d-sdk.
Interrupted after the same Cubism SDK download hook stalled before test execution.

tests.coverage: Rust tests cover display-centered placement, explicit coordinate precedence, no-monitor fallback, oversized-window display origin behavior, and physical-to-logical display conversion for named and unnamed monitors.

discoveredIssues.description: Managed windows without explicit coordinates used Tauri global center(), which can place windows on the primary monitor instead of the display where AIRI is active. Fixed by centering on the main/current monitor work area.
Tauri screen commands returned a fake 1920x1080 primary display and origin cursor point. Fixed by using Tauri monitor and cursor APIs with logical coordinate conversion.
Manual always-on-top and multi-display screenshots remain pending.
The external @proj-airi/unplugin-live2d-sdk has no timeout or skip flag and stalled during cold-cache Vite/Vitest runs. Existing mission handoff already notes cold-cache Cubism SDK fragility.
Serena/jcodemunch MCP tools required by AGENTS.md were unavailable in this session, so native tooling fallback was used.

deviations.step: AGENTS.md MCP startup and delegated code-change loop
deviations.whatIDidInstead: Used native rg/sed/git/apply_patch/pnpm/cargo tooling directly from the isolated worktree.
deviations.why: The MCP tools named by AGENTS.md were not exposed in the current tool list.

deviations.whatIDidInstead: Ran Rust tests/build, rustfmt, whitespace, and package typecheck; recorded build/Vitest stall as pending runtime evidence.
deviations.why: Vite/Vitest stalled in the external Cubism SDK download hook and generated asset paths were not ignored, so prewarming the cache would have dirtied the worktree.

suggestedChanges: Add a timeout or skip/cache option to @proj-airi/unplugin-live2d-sdk so build/test verification cannot hang indefinitely on cold cache.
